chapters.name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: According to the Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic, if 40 = x^y · z, then find the values of x, y and z.
Prove that 6 + √2 is an irrational number.
questions.answer: Assume 6 + √2 is rational. Then √2 = (6+√2) - 6 is rational. But √2 is irrational — contradiction. Hence 6 + √2 is irrational.

questions.question: Prove that √5 is an irrational number.
questions.answer: Assume √5 = p/q (lowest terms). Then 5q² = p², so 5 | p². By Euclid's lemma 5 | p. Let p = 5m. Then q² = 5m², so 5 | q. Both p, q divisible by 5 — contradicts lowest terms. Hence √5 is irrational.

questions.question: Prove that 2 + √5 is an irrational number.
questions.answer: Assume 2 + √5 is rational. Then √5 = (2+√5) - 2 is rational. But √5 is irrational — contradiction. Hence 2 + √5 is irrational.

questions.question: Prove that 2 + √3 is an irrational number.
questions.answer: Assume 2 + √3 is rational. Then √3 is rational — contradiction. Hence 2 + √3 is irrational.

questions.question: Prove that 5 − √2 is an irrational number.
questions.answer: Assume 5 − √2 is rational. Then √2 = 5 − (5−√2) is rational — contradiction. Hence 5 − √2 is irrational.

questions.question: The HCF and LCM of two positive integers are 4 and 60 respectively. If one of the integers is 20, find the other integer.
questions.answer: Product of two numbers = HCF × LCM = 4 × 60 = 240. Other number = 240 ÷ 20 = 12.

questions.question: The product of HCF and LCM of two numbers is 2016. If one of the numbers is 42, find the other number. Also find the HCF by prime factorisation method.
If the product of LCM and HCF of two numbers is 2366 and their HCF is 13, find their LCM. If one of the numbers is 91, find the other number.
The H.C.F of 3 and 5 is,
If p and q are co-primes, then the H.C.F of p² and q² is,
Find the H.C.F. of 135 and 75 by prime factorization method and then find the L.C.M. of 20 and H.C.F(135, 75).

The lengths of two rectangular tables are 84cm and 126cm respectively and both have a width equal to 42cm. Find the maximum length of a square glass sheet that can exactly fit both the tables. Also calculate the total number of glass sheets required to fit each table.
questions.answer: HCF(84, 126, 42) = 42. Max square size = 42cm. Table 1: 84×42 → 84/42 × 42/42 = 2×1 = 2 sheets. Table 2: 126×42 → 3×1 = 3 sheets.

questions.question: In an arithmetic progression, if S20 = 820 and a20 = 79, then find its first term.
questions.note: This question is from Chapter 5 context but appears as a 2-mark in MQP1 Section III

questions.question: Find the zeroes of the quadratic polynomial p(x) = x² + 7x + 10 and verify the relationship between the zeroes and the coefficients.
questions.answer: x² + 7x + 10 = (x+2)(x+5). Zeroes: α = −2, β = −5. Sum = −7 = −b/a ✓. Product = 10 = c/a ✓.

questions.question: Find the zeroes of the quadratic polynomial p(x) = 6x² − 7x − 3 and verify the relationship between the zeroes and the coefficients.
In the figure, the number of zeroes of the polynomial y = p(x) is (graph shows curve crossing x-axis at 3 points and touching at 1),
If the zeroes of the polynomial p(x) = x² + 3x + k are reciprocal to each other, then find the value of k.
questions.answer: If zeroes are α and 1/α, product = α × (1/α) = 1 = k/1 = k. Therefore k = 1.

questions.question: Write the quadratic polynomial whose sum and product of zeroes are 2 and 8 respectively and hence find the zeroes of the polynomial.
questions.answer: p(x) = x² − (sum)x + product = x² − 2x + 8. Discriminant = 4 − 32 = −28 < 0. No real zeroes. (Note: If sum=−2, product=−8: p(x)=x²+2x−8=(x+4)(x−2), zeroes: −4, 2)

questions.question: If α and β are the zeroes of the quadratic polynomial p(x) = x² + 3x + 1, find the value of (i) α/β + β/α (ii) α² + β²
questions.answer: Sum α+β = −3, Product αβ = 1. (i) α/β + β/α = (α²+β²)/αβ = [(α+β)²−2αβ]/αβ = [9−2]/1 = 7. (ii) α²+β² = (α+β)²−2αβ = 9−2 = 7.

questions.question: If α and β are the two zeroes of the polynomial p(x) = x² + 3x + 2, then find the value of α³β + αβ³.
If one of the zeroes of the polynomial P(x) = 2x² − 8x + (k+1) is seven times the other, then find the value of k.
questions.answer: Let zeroes be α and 7α. Sum = 8α = 8/2 = 4, so α = 1/2. Product = 7α² = 7/4 = (k+1)/2. So k+1 = 7/2, k = 5/2.

questions.question: The quadratic equation in the following is,
Write the degree of a linear polynomial.

questions.question: If the lines represented by the equations a₁x + b₁y + c₁ = 0 and a₂x + b₂y + c₂ = 0 are coincident, then the correct relation is,

questions.question: In the pair of linear equations a₁x + b₁y + c₁ = 0 and a₂x + b₂y + c₂ = 0, if a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ ≠ c₁/c₂ then the lines represented by them are,

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: If the pair of linear equations a₁x + b₁y + c₁ = 0 and a₂x + b₂y + c₂ = 0 have unique solution, then the correct relation is,

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: How many solutions do the pair of linear equations 2x + 3y − 9 = 0 and 3x + 2y − 6 = 0 have?
questions.answer: a₁/a₂ = 2/3, b₁/b₂ = 3/2. Since 2/3 ≠ 3/2, lines are intersecting. The pair has exactly one (unique) solution.

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Solve the given pair of linear equations by elimination method: 2x + y = 10, x − y = 2
questions.answer: Adding: 3x = 12, x = 4. Substituting: 2(4) + y = 10, y = 2. Solution: x = 4, y = 2.

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Solve the given pair of linear equations by elimination method: x + 2y = 10, x + y = 6
questions.answer: Subtracting: y = 4. x = 6 − 4 = 2. Solution: x = 2, y = 4.

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Solve the given pair of linear equations: 3x − y = 15, 2x − y = 5
questions.answer: Subtracting: x = 10. y = 3(10) − 15 = 15. Solution: x = 10, y = 15.

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Find the solution of the given pair of linear equations by graphical method: x + 2y = 8, x + y = 5
questions.answer: For x + 2y = 8: points (0,4), (8,0), (2,3). For x + y = 5: points (0,5), (5,0), (2,3). Lines intersect at (2, 3). Solution: x = 2, y = 3.

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Solve the given pair of linear equations by graphical method: 2x + y = 8, x − y = 1

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Solve the given pair of linear equations by graphical method: x + 2y = 6, x + y = 5

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Solve the given pair of linear equations by elimination method: 2x + y = 10, x − y = 2
questions.answer: Adding: 3x = 12, x = 4. y = 10 − 2(4) = 2. Solution: x = 4, y = 2.

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Solve the given pair of linear equations using suitable method: 2x + y = 8, 3x − y = 7
questions.answer: Adding: 5x = 15, x = 3. y = 8 − 2(3) = 2. Solution: x = 3, y = 2.

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Find the solution of the given pair of linear equations by graphical method: 3x + y = 10, x + y = 6
Find the roots of the quadratic equation x² + 8x + 12 = 0.
Find the discriminant of the quadratic equation x² + 4x + 5 = 0 and hence write the nature of the roots.
questions.answer: D = b² − 4ac = 16 − 20 = −4. Since D < 0, the equation has no real roots (complex roots).

questions.question: Find the discriminant of the quadratic equation 2x² + 3x − 7 = 0 and determine the nature of roots.
questions.answer: D = 9 + 56 = 65. Since D > 0, the equation has two distinct real roots.

questions.question: Find the roots of the quadratic equation 2x² + x − 6 = 0.
For what value of k the roots of the equation kx² + 6x + 1 = 0 are equal?
questions.answer: For equal roots, D = 0. b² − 4ac = 36 − 4k = 0. k = 9.

questions.question: Find the roots of the quadratic equation x² − 3x − 10 = 0.
The difference between the altitude and base of a right angled triangle is 5 cm. If the area of the triangle is 150 cm², then find the base and altitude of the triangle.
questions.answer: Let base = x, altitude = x + 5. Area = x(x+5)/2 = 150. x² + 5x − 300 = 0. (x+20)(x−15) = 0. x = 15 (taking positive). Base = 15 cm, Altitude = 20 cm.

questions.question: The sum of the squares of two consecutive even positive integers is 164. Find the integers.
questions.answer: Let integers be x and x+2. x² + (x+2)² = 164. 2x² + 4x + 4 = 164. x² + 2x − 80 = 0. (x+10)(x−8) = 0. x = 8. Integers: 8 and 10.

questions.question: An express train takes 1 hour less than a passenger train to travel a distance of 132 km. The average speed of the express train is 11 km/h more than that of the passenger train. Find the average speed of these two trains.
questions.answer: Let speed of passenger train = x km/h. Express train speed = x+11. Time difference: 132/x − 132/(x+11) = 1. 132(x+11) − 132x = x(x+11). 1452 = x²+11x. x²+11x−1452=0. (x+44)(x−33)=0. x=33. Passenger: 33 km/h, Express: 44 km/h.

questions.question: The denominator of a fraction is one more than twice its numerator. The sum of this fraction and its reciprocal is 29/10. Find the fraction.

The duration of the spacewalk of astronaut A is 6 hours longer than the duration of the spacewalk of astronaut B. If they both continue spacewalk for 4 more hours, the product of their spacewalk duration becomes 720 hours. Find the spacewalk durations of astronauts A and B.
Find the discriminant of the quadratic equation 2x² − 6x + 3 = 0 and write the nature of roots.
questions.answer: D = 36 − 24 = 12. Since D > 0, two distinct real roots.

questions.question: The common difference of the arithmetic progression −1, −3, −5, ... is,
Find the sum of first 20 terms of the arithmetic progression 5, 9, 13, ... using formula.
Find the sum of the first 20 terms of the arithmetic progression 6, 10, 14, ... using formula.
Find the sum of first 20 terms of the arithmetic progression 11, 15, 19, 23, ...

An arithmetic progression consists of 16 terms. The sum of all its terms is 768. If the last term of the progression is 93, then find the arithmetic progression. Also show that the sum of all the terms of this progression is equal to 3 times the sum of first 16 odd natural numbers using formula.
In an arithmetic progression, the seventh term is 12 more than its fourth term. Find the common difference.
If the first term of an arithmetic progression is 'm' and the common difference is 3, then find the 10th term of the progression.
The sum of the first 6 terms of an arithmetic progression is 42. If the ratio of the 10th term to the 30th term of the progression is 1:3, then find the 13th term of the progression.
The sum of first n terms of an arithmetic progression is Sn = 3n² + 2n. Find the nth term of the progression. If this progression consists of 59 terms, find the sum of the last 10 terms.
If the first term of an arithmetic progression is 1 and the 14th term is 53, then find the nth term of this progression.
Find how many two digit numbers are divisible by 3.
questions.answer: First 2-digit multiple of 3: 12. Last: 99. aₙ = 12+(n−1)3 = 99. n = 30.

questions.question: How many numbers between 201 and 401 are divisible by 6?
In the following, the shapes which are always similar are,
In the given figure, write the similarity criterion used to show that △ABC ~ △QRP (sides 2, 3, 2.5 and 6, 5, 4 are given proportionally).
questions.answer: SSS (Side-Side-Side) similarity criterion. AB/QR = BC/RP = CA/PQ = 1/2.

questions.question: Prove that 'The ratio of the areas of two similar triangles is equal to the square of the ratio of their corresponding sides'.
questions.answer: Given: △ABC ~ △PQR. Draw AM ⊥ BC, PN ⊥ QR. Area(△ABC)/Area(△PQR) = (1/2×BC×AM)/(1/2×QR×PN). AM/PN = AB/PQ (AA similarity of △ABM and △PQN). Since △ABC~△PQR, AB/PQ = BC/QR. So ratio = (BC/QR)×(AB/PQ) = (AB/PQ)². Hence proved.

questions.question: A ladder 10 m long reaches a window 8 m above the ground. Find the distance of the foot of the ladder from the base of the wall.
questions.answer: By Pythagoras theorem: d² + 8² = 10². d² = 100−64 = 36. d = 6 m.

questions.question: ABC is a right angled triangle. If ∠B = 90° and AD ⊥ BC, then show that AB² = BC × BD.
questions.answer: In △ABD and △ABC: ∠ADB = ∠ABC = 90°. ∠ABD = ∠BAC (complementary angles). By AA similarity, △ABD ~ △CBA. Therefore AB/CB = BD/AB. Hence AB² = CB × BD.

questions.question: In the figure, DE ∥ BC. If AD = 2 cm and BD = 6 cm, then find DE:BC.
questions.answer: By BPT: AD/AB = DE/BC. AB = 8. DE/BC = 2/8 = 1/4. DE:BC = 1:4.

questions.question: Prove that 'If a line is drawn parallel to one side of a triangle to intersect the other two sides in distinct points, the other two sides are divided in the same ratio' (Basic Proportionality Theorem).
questions.answer: Given: In △ABC, DE ∥ BC, D on AB, E on AC. Draw DN ⊥ AE, EM ⊥ AD. Area(△ADE)/Area(△BDE) = AD/BD (same height). Area(△ADE)/Area(△CED) = AE/CE. But △BDE and △CED have equal areas (same base DE, equal heights). So AD/BD = AE/CE. Hence proved.

questions.question: In the figure, DE ∥ BC. If BD = 7.2 cm, AE = 1.8 cm and EC = 5.4 cm, then find the length of AD.

Prove that 'If in two triangles, sides of one triangle are proportional to the sides of the other triangle, then their corresponding angles are equal and hence the two triangles are similar' (SSS similarity criterion).
questions.answer: Given △ABC and △DEF with AB/DE = BC/EF = CA/FD. Construct △DEF' ≅ △ABC. Show by SAS and congruence that all angles match, hence △ABC ~ △DEF.

questions.question: In the given figure, the secant of the circle is,
Prove that 'The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact'.
questions.answer: Let O be the centre, P the point of contact, PT the tangent. For any point Q on tangent other than P, OQ > OP (Q is outside circle). So OP is the shortest distance from O to line PT. Hence OP ⊥ PT.

questions.question: Prove that 'The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal'.
questions.answer: Let P be external point. PA and PB are tangents touching at A and B. Join OA, OB, OP. In △OAP and △OBP: OA = OB (radii), OP common, ∠OAP = ∠OBP = 90° (tangent ⊥ radius). By RHS congruence △OAP ≅ △OBP. Hence PA = PB.

questions.question: In the given figure O is the centre of the circle. If ∠AOB = 90° and OA = 7 cm, then the length of the arc AMB is,
In the given figure, PA and PB are tangents to the circle with centre O. If PA = 4 cm and ∠APO = 40°, then find the measure of ∠AOB and length of PB.
Two concentric circles of radii 5 cm and 3 cm are drawn with centre O. Find the length of the chord of the larger circle which touches the smaller circle at point P.
questions.answer: OP = 3 (radius of smaller circle), OA = 5 (radius of larger circle). AP² = OA² − OP² = 25−9 = 16. AP = 4. Chord AB = 2AP = 8 cm.

questions.question: If A(1, y), B(4, 3), C(x, 6) and D(3, 5) are the vertices of a parallelogram taken in order, then find the values of x and y.
questions.answer: Diagonals bisect each other. Midpoint of AC = Midpoint of BD. (1+x)/2 = (4+3)/2 → x = 6. (y+6)/2 = (3+5)/2 → y = 2.

questions.question: Find the coordinates of a point which divides the line segment joining the points (−1, 7) and (4, −3) internally in the ratio 2:3.
Find a relation between x and y such that the point (x, y) is equidistant from the points (3, 6) and (−3, 4).
Find the coordinates of the point which divides the line segment joining the points (1, 6) and (4, 3) in the ratio 1:2 internally.
If the coordinates of the midpoint of the line segment joining the points A(4, 6) and B(x, y) are (6, 5), then find the coordinates of point B.
Find the coordinates of the centre of a circle passing through the points A(3, 0), B(0, 2) and C(0, 3).
questions.answer: Let centre = (h,k). (h−3)²+k² = h²+(k−2)² = h²+(k−3)². From 2nd and 3rd: (k−2)² = (k−3)² → k = 5/2. From 1st and 2nd: h²−6h+9+k² = h²+k²−4k+4 → −6h+9 = −4(5/2)+4 = −6 → 6h = 15 → h = 5/2. Centre: (5/2, 5/2).

questions.question: The distance of a point P(x, y) from the origin is,
Find the coordinates of the points of trisection of the line segment joining the points (2, −2) and (−7, 4).
If P(E) = 0.05, then P(Ē) is equal to,
questions.note: This appears in probability context but is a 1-mark question

questions.question: If sin θ = 12/15, then write the value of cosec θ.
Prove that: cosA/(1+sinA) + (1+sinA)/cosA = 2 secA.
Find the value of: (5cos²60° + 4sec²30° − tan²45°) / (sin²30° + cos²30°)
In the figure, find the value of tan P and cos R (right triangle with PQ = 12 cm, QR = 5 cm, PR = 13 cm).
questions.answer: tan P = QR/PQ = 5/12. cos R = QR/PR = 5/13.

questions.question: Prove that: (1 + cosA)(1 − cosA) = sin²A   [OR]   Prove that: √(1−sinA)/(1+sinA) = cosecA − cotA
Prove that: (sinA + cosecA)² + (cosA + secA)² = 7 + tan²A + cot²A
If tan A = √3, then the measure of angle A is,
Evaluate: tan60°/cot60° + sec60°/(2sin90°) − 1/cos²45°
If sinA + sin²A = 1, then find the value of cos²A + cos⁴A.
questions.answer: sinA = 1 − sin²A = cos²A. So cos²A + cos⁴A = sinA + sin²A = 1.

questions.question: A pole and a tower are standing vertically on a level ground. The height of the pole is 6 m and the angle of elevation to the top of the pole from the bottom of the tower is 30°. The angle of elevation to the top of the tower from the top of the pole is 60°. Find the height of the tower (CD) and the distance (AC) between the top of the pole and the top of the tower.
questions.answer: Let BD = distance between them. tan30° = AB/BD = 6/BD → BD = 6√3. CE = BD = 6√3. tan60° = CE/AE → CE = AE×√3. AE = BD = 6√3. CE = 6√3×√3 = 18. CD = CE + ED = 18 + 6 = 24 m. AC = √(AE²+CE²) = √(108+324) = √432 = 12√3 m.

questions.question: A straight highway leads to the foot of a tower. A person at the top observes a car at point C with angle of depression 60°, moving away. Six seconds later, the person observes the car at point B with angle of depression 30°. If height of tower is 30 m, find the time taken by the car to reach point B from the foot, and the distance between the top of the tower and point B.

A tower and a building stand vertically on level ground. From the foot of the tower, the angle of elevation to a window of the building 20 m above the ground is 30°. From the same window, the angle of elevation to the top of the tower is 60°. From the top of the building, the angle of depression to the foot of the tower is 45°. Find the distance between them, height of tower, height of building, and distance from top of building to foot of tower.
questions.answer: From foot of tower: tan30° = 20/d → d = 20√3. From window: tan60° = (h−20)/d → h−20 = 20√3×√3 = 60 → h = 80 m. From top of building: tan45° = H/d → H = d = 20√3. Wait — need to recalculate. Let d = distance. tan30° = 20/d, d = 20√3. tan60° = (h−20)/20√3 → h = 80. Top of building angle: given 45°, building height = H. tan45° = H/d → H = 20√3. Distance from top of building to foot = √(d²+H²) = √(1200+1200) = 20√6.

questions.question: The lighthouse AB of height 10√3 m stands on a sea shore. A tower CE and a ship F are standing 30 m and 10 m away from the foot of the lighthouse respectively. The angle of elevation of the top of the tower from the top of the lighthouse is 30°. Find the height of the tower and distance AE. Also find the angle of depression from the top of the lighthouse to the ship.
questions.answer: BC = 30 m. tan30° = DE/BC → DE = 30/√3 = 10. CE = DE + CD = 10 + 10√3. AE = √(AB²+BC²) = √(300+900) = √1200 = 20√3. For ship: BF = 10. tan(depression) = AB/BF = 10√3/10 = √3 → angle = 60°.

questions.question: The volume of a sphere of radius 'r' units is,
Write the formula to find the total surface area of a cube of edge 'a' units.
questions.answer: Total surface area of cube = 6a² square units.

questions.question: A solid consisting of a right circular cone of height 120 cm and radius 60 cm standing on a hemisphere of radius 60 cm is placed upright in a right circular cylinder full of water such that it touches the bottom. If the radius of the cylinder is 60 cm and height is 180 cm, find the volume of water left in the cylinder in terms of π.
questions.answer: Volume of cylinder = π×60²×180 = 648000π. Volume of cone = (1/3)π×60²×120 = 144000π. Volume of hemisphere = (2/3)π×60³ = 144000π. Volume of solid = 288000π. Water left = 648000π − 288000π = 360000π cm³.

questions.question: A hollow solid is made by placing a cylinder on an inverted cone. The radius of the cylinder and the cone are 7 cm and their heights are 12 cm and 9 cm respectively. The solid is filled with water up to 2/3rd the height of the cylinder. Find the amount of water in the solid. Calculate the amount of water required to fill the solid completely.
questions.answer: Volume of cylinder = π×49×12 = 588π. Volume up to 2/3 height = π×49×8 = 392π. Volume of cone = (1/3)π×49×9 = 147π. Total capacity = 588π + 147π = 735π. Water already = 392π. Water to fill completely = 735π − 392π = 343π ≈ 1077.57 cm³.

questions.question: From a wooden cylinder of height 18 cm and base diameter 7 cm, a hemisphere and a cone having the same radius as that of the cylinder are carved out. If the height of the cone is 12 cm, find the cost of painting the solid formed at the rate of ₹2.50 per cm².
questions.answer: r = 3.5, h_cyl = 18, h_cone = 12. CSA of cylinder = 2πrh = 2π(3.5)(18) = 126π. Base of cylinder = πr² = 12.25π. Curved surface of cone = πrl = π(3.5)(√(12.25+144)) = π(3.5)(√156.25) = π(3.5)(12.5) = 43.75π. CSA of hemisphere = 2πr² = 24.5π. Total SA = 126π + 12.25π + 43.75π + 24.5π = 206.5π = 648.43 cm². Cost = 648.43 × 2.50 = ₹1621.07.

questions.question: A solid is made by joining a cylinder, a hemisphere and a cone of equal radii. The height of the cylinder is 6 cm and the total height of the solid is 13 cm. If the volume of the cylinder is three times the volume of the hemisphere, find the volume and surface area of the solid.
questions.answer: Volume of cylinder = 3 × volume of hemisphere. πr²h = 3×(2/3)πr³ → 6 = 2r → r = 3. Total height 13: r(hemisphere)=3, h_cylinder=6, h_cone=13−3−6=4. l_cone = √(9+16) = 5. Volume = πr²h + (2/3)πr³ + (1/3)πr²h_cone = 9π(6) + (2/3)(27π) + (1/3)(9π)(4) = 54π+18π+12π = 84π cm³. SA = CSA_cylinder + hemisphere + cone = 2π(3)(6) + 2π(9) + π(3)(5) = 36π+18π+15π = 69π cm².

questions.question: Find the surface area of a sphere of radius 7 cm.
The circumference of the base of a cylinder is 10 cm and its height is 25 cm. Find its curved surface area.
In the given figure O is the centre of the circle of radius 21 cm. If ∠AOB = 60°, find the area of the segment APB. [Take √3 = 1.73]
questions.answer: Area of sector = (60/360)×π×21² = (1/6)×(22/7)×441 = 231 cm². Area of triangle AOB = (√3/4)×21² = (1.73/4)×441 = 190.6 cm². Area of segment = 231 − 190.6 = 40.4 cm².

questions.question: A hand fan is made in the shape of a sector of a circle of radius 21 cm and angle 120°. Calculate the area of the cloth used and the total length of the metallic wire required.
The minute hand of a wall clock is 18 cm long. Find the area swept by the minute hand in 35 minutes. Find the length of the arc formed by the sweep of the minute hand.
In the figure O is the centre of the circle. Area of sector AOB : Area of the circle = 1:5 and if the radius of the circle is 7 cm, then find the length of arc AB.
In the figure O is the centre of the circle with radius 21 cm. The length of the arc APB is 22 cm. Find the area of the segment APB.
In the given frequency distribution table, write the mid-point of the modal class: Class 1–3 (f=4), 3–5 (f=8), 5–7 (f=2), 7–9 (f=2).
Find the mean for the following data: Class 10–20 (f=2), 20–30 (f=3), 30–40 (f=6), 40–50 (f=5), 50–60 (f=4).
Find the median for the following data: Class 15–20 (f=4), 20–25 (f=5), 25–30 (f=10), 30–35 (f=5), 35–40 (f=6).
questions.answer: n = 30, n/2 = 15. Cumulative frequencies: 4, 9, 19, 24, 30. Median class = 25–30. Median = 25 + [(15−9)/10]×5 = 25 + 3 = 28.

questions.question: The following data gives the weights of 50 students. Draw a 'less than type ogive' for the data: Less than 38(0), 40(5), 42(10), 44(25), 46(35), 48(40), 50(50).
questions.answer: Plot points (38,0), (40,5), (42,10), (44,25), (46,35), (48,40), (50,50) on a graph. Join with a smooth curve. The curve represents less than type ogive.

questions.question: Find the Mode for the following frequency distribution: Class 10–20 (f=6), 20–30 (f=4), 30–40 (f=12), 40–50 (f=3), 50–60 (f=5).
Find the Mean for the following frequency distribution: Class 0–10 (f=4), 10–20 (f=9), 20–30 (f=15), 30–40 (f=14), 40–50 (f=8).
Find the mean for the following frequency distribution: Class 5–15 (f=2), 15–25 (f=3), 25–35 (f=5), 35–45 (f=7), 45–55 (f=3).
If P(E) = 0.05, then P(Ē) is equal to,
If two fair coins are tossed simultaneously, then what is the probability of getting two heads?
questions.answer: Sample space = {HH, HT, TH, TT}. n(S) = 4. P(HH) = 1/4.

questions.question: A box contains cards numbered from 6 to 70. If one card is drawn at random, find the probability that the card will have: (i) a perfect square number (ii) a number divisible by 5 (iii) an odd number less than 30.
A box contains 20 cards numbered from 1 to 20. One card is drawn randomly. Find the probability of getting: (i) a perfect square number (ii) a number which is divisible by both 2 and 3.
questions.answer: (i) Perfect squares: 1,4,9,16 = 4. P = 4/20 = 1/5. (ii) Divisible by 6: 6,12,18 = 3. P = 3/20.

questions.question: Three fair coins are tossed simultaneously. Find the probability of getting: (i) at least two heads (ii) at most two tails.
questions.answer: Sample space: 8 outcomes. (i) At least 2H: HHH, HHT, HTH, THH = 4. P = 4/8 = 1/2. (ii) At most 2 tails: all except TTT = 7. P = 7/8.

questions.question: If two fair dice are rolled simultaneously, find the probability (a) that the sum of numbers on the top faces is less than 8 (b) of getting a square number on one of the faces only.
questions.answer: Total outcomes = 36. (a) Sum < 8: count pairs (1,1) to (6,1) etc = 21 outcomes. P = 21/36 = 7/12. (b) Square numbers on dice: 1, 4. One face is square, other is not square: 2×(2×4) = 16. P = 16/36 = 4/9.

questions.question: A bag contains 20 red balls and some blue balls. If the probability of drawing a blue ball is 1/4th the probability of drawing a red ball, find the number of blue balls and total number of balls in the bag.
Write the value of P(E) + P(Ē) with reference to probability.

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Solve the given pair of linear equations by graphical method: 2x + y = 6, x + y = 4

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Solve the given pair of linear equations by graphical method: x + y = 5, 2x − y = 4

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: Solve the given pair of linear equations by graphical method: 3x + 2y = 12, x − y = 1
questions.answer: For 3x+2y=12: (0,6),(4,0),(2,3). For x−y=1: (0,−1),(1,0),(2,1). Hmm. Let me recalculate for intersection: 3x+2y=12 and x−y=1 → x=y+1 → 3(y+1)+2y=12 → 5y=9 → y=9/5=1.8, x=2.8. Plot both lines and mark intersection at (2.8, 1.8).

questions.question: The 10th term of an arithmetic progression is 2 more than three times of the first term and the 8th term is 6 more than twice the first term. Find how many terms of the progression make the sum 540.
In an arithmetic progression, the sum of 4th term and 8th terms is 24, and sum of 6th term and 10th terms is 44. Find the arithmetic progression and also find the 25th term.
An arithmetic progression consists of four terms whose sum is 32. The ratio of the product of first and last terms to the product of second and third terms is 7:15. Find the terms of the progression.

A book contains 432 pages. A student reads 25 pages on the first day and reads 2 more pages each subsequent day. Find how many pages he will read on the 12th day and how many days to read the book completely.
Prove that 'If in two triangles, corresponding angles are equal, then their corresponding sides are in the same ratio and hence the two triangles are similar' (AA Similarity / AAA criterion).
questions.answer: Given: In △ABC and △DEF, ∠A=∠D, ∠B=∠E. To prove: △ABC~△DEF. Construct △DEF' where DF'=AC and ∠EDF'=∠A. Then △DEF'≅△ABC (AAS). So ∠DEF'=∠B=∠E. So EF' ∥ EF (corresponding angles equal). By BPT, DF'/DF = DE/DE. Hence all sides proportional. ∠F'=∠F. Therefore △ABC~△DEF. Hence proved.

questions.question: Prove that 'If one angle of a triangle is equal to one angle of the other triangle and the sides including these angles are proportional, then the two triangles are similar' (SAS Similarity).
questions.answer: Given: In △ABC and △DEF, ∠A=∠D and AB/DE=AC/DF. To prove: △ABC~△DEF. Take point P on DE such that DP=AB and Q on DF such that DQ=AC. Then △DPQ≅△ABC (SAS). ∠DPQ=∠B. Since DP/DE=DQ/DF, by converse of BPT, PQ∥EF. ∠DPQ=∠E, so ∠B=∠E. Similarly ∠C=∠F. By AA, △ABC~△DEF. Proved.

questions.question: Two line segments AB and CD intersect each other at point O. Join AC and BD such that AC ∥ BD. Prove that △AOC ~ △BOD.
questions.answer: Given: AB and CD intersect at O. AC ∥ BD. In △AOC and △BOD: ∠OAC = ∠OBD (alternate interior angles, AC∥BD). ∠AOC = ∠BOD (vertically opposite angles). By AA similarity criterion, △AOC ~ △BOD. Hence OA/OB = OC/OD = AC/BD. Proved.

questions.question: Two solid cones of radii equal to the base radius of the cylinder are hollowed out from it. The ratio of volumes of cones is 2:1. The diameter and height of the cylinder are 6 cm and 21 cm respectively. Find the height and volume of cones. Also find the volume of the remaining portion.
questions.answer: r=3, H=21. h₁:h₂=2:1, h₁+h₂=21, so h₁=14, h₂=7. V₁=(1/3)π(9)(14)=42π. V₂=(1/3)π(9)(7)=21π. Total cone volume=63π. Cylinder volume=π×9×21=189π. Remaining=189π−63π=126π≈395.84 cm³.

questions.question: A solid toy is made by joining a right circular cone of height 12 cm to a hemisphere of base radius 3.5 cm. The toy is immersed in a cylindrical glass vessel completely filled with water. If the diameter of the cylinder is 14 cm and its height equals the height of the toy, find the volume of water left in the cylinder and total surface area of the toy.

A vessel is in the form of an inverted cone with height 8 cm and radius 5 cm. The vessel is filled with water to the brim and when 100 lead shots of same size are dropped in it, 1/4th of the water flows out. Find the radius and volume of each lead shot.
questions.answer: Volume of cone=(1/3)π(25)(8)=200π/3. Water out=200π/12=50π/3. This equals volume of 100 lead shots. Each shot=(50π/3)/100=π/6. (4/3)πr³=π/6. r³=1/8. r=0.5 cm. Volume each=π/6≈0.524 cm³.

questions.question: A rectangular wooden block has length 40 cm, breadth 30 cm and height 7.5 cm. From this block, a cone of radius 10 cm is drilled out. Find the volume and surface area of the remaining solid.
questions.answer: V_block=40×30×7.5=9000 cm³. h_cone=7.5, r=10. l=√(10²+7.5²)=√156.25=12.5. V_cone=(1/3)π(100)(7.5)=250π≈785.7. V_remaining=9000−250π≈8214.3 cm³. SA_block=2(40×30+40×7.5+30×7.5)=2(1200+300+225)=3450. Hole: top circle area=πr²=100π removed. CSA of cone=πrl=125π added. Bottom of cone open (going through)=100π. Net SA=3450−100π+125π=3450+25π≈3528.5 cm².

questions.question: Find the Mode for the following frequency distribution: Class 15–20 (f=6), 20–25 (f=9), 25–30 (f=15), 30–35 (f=9), 35–40 (f=1).
Find the Median for the following frequency distribution: Class 10–20 (f=6), 20–30 (f=4), 30–40 (f=12), 40–50 (f=3), 50–60 (f=5).

The following frequency distribution gives the daily income of 50 workers. Draw a 'less than type ogive' and hence find the median: Class 100–120 (f=12), 120–140 (f=14), 140–160 (f=8), 160–180 (f=6), 180–200 (f=10).
Draw a 'more than type ogive' for the following data and find the median: Class 0–10 (f=5), 10–20 (f=8), 20–30 (f=15), 30–40 (f=10), 40–50 (f=7), 50–60 (f=5).

A building (AD) and a tower (BE) are standing vertically on level ground. From the top of the building, a drone travels for 10s and reaches the top of the tower along straight line AB with angle of elevation 30°. The angle of elevation from the bottom of the building to the top of the tower is 60° and the distance BD = 10√3 m. Find the height of building, height of tower, distance DE between them and speed of drone.
questions.answer: Let height of building AD=h₁, tower BE=h₂, distance BD=10√3. From bottom D: tan60°=BE/DE → h₂/DE=√3. From top A: tan30°=CE/AE → CE/AE=1/√3 (CE=BE−AD=h₂−h₁, AE=DE). So (h₂−h₁)/DE=1/√3. From tan60°: h₂=DE×√3. From tan30°: h₂−h₁=DE/√3. h₁=DE×√3−DE/√3=2DE/√3. Also from tan30° of foot: BD/AD=cot30°=√3? Wait. Let DE=d. tan60°=h₂/d → h₂=d√3. AE=d. AB (drone path) from top A to top B. tan30° from A to B: (h₂−h₁)/d=1/√3. h₂−h₁=d/√3. h₁=d√3−d/√3=2d/√3. BD=10√3=d. d=10√3. h₂=10√3×√3=30m. h₁=2×10√3/√3=20m. DE=10√3≈17.3m. AB=√(d²+(h₂−h₁)²)=√(300+100/3)=√(1000/3)=10√(10/3). Speed=AB/10.

questions.question: The vertices of △ABC are A(7,2), B(9,10) and C(1,4). E and F are midpoints of AB and AC respectively. Prove that EF = ½ BC using distance formula.
Draw a circle of radius 4 cm. From a point 9 cm away from its centre, construct two tangents to the circle and measure their lengths.
questions.answer: Steps: 1. Draw circle with centre O, radius 4 cm. 2. Mark point P such that OP=9 cm. 3. Draw perpendicular bisector of OP; find midpoint M. 4. Draw circle with centre M, radius MP=OP/2=4.5 cm. 5. This circle intersects first circle at A and B. 6. PA and PB are the required tangents. Length = √(OP²−r²) = √(81−16) = √65 ≈ 8.06 cm.

questions.question: Construct a triangle with sides 6 cm, 7.5 cm and 9 cm and then construct another triangle whose sides are 2/3 of the corresponding sides of the first triangle.
questions.answer: Steps: 1. Draw BC=9 cm. 2. With B as centre, radius 6 cm draw arc. With C as centre, radius 7.5 cm draw arc. Mark intersection as A. △ABC is the required triangle. 3. Draw ray BX below BC. Mark 3 equal parts on BX: B₁B₂B₃. 4. Join B₃C. Draw line through B₂ parallel to B₃C meeting BC at C'. 5. Draw line through C' parallel to CA meeting BA at A'. 6. △A'BC' is the required triangle with sides 2/3 of △ABC.

questions.question: The ages of two students A and B are 19 years and 15 years respectively. Find how many years it will take so that the product of their ages becomes 480.

A cottage industry produces a certain number of pottery articles in a day. The cost of production of each article (in rupees) was 3 more than twice the number of articles produced. If the total cost of production was ₹90, find the number of articles produced and cost of each article.
questions.answer: Let number of articles = x. Cost per article = 2x+3. Total cost = x(2x+3) = 90. 2x²+3x−90=0. Discriminant=9+720=729. x=(−3+27)/4=6. Articles=6, Cost=2(6)+3=₹15.

The degree of a cubic polynomial is,
The formula to find the midpoint of a class interval is,

questions.chapter_name: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
questions.question: If the lines representing the pair of linear equations a₁x+b₁y+c₁=0 and a₂x+b₂y+c₂=0 intersect each other, then the correct relation is,
Find the zeroes of the polynomial p(x) = x² − 5 and verify the relationship between zeroes and coefficients.
questions.answer: x²=5 → x=±√5. Zeroes: α=√5, β=−√5. Sum=0=−b/a=0 ✓. Product=−5=c/a=−5 ✓.

questions.question: Express the quadratic equation (2x+3)x = x² + 1 in standard form.
questions.answer: 2x²+3x = x²+1. 2x²+3x−x²−1=0. x²+3x−1=0. Standard form: x²+3x−1=0, where a=1, b=3, c=−1.

questions.question: Find which term of the arithmetic progression 2, 7, 12, ... is 47 using formula.
If (sec²θ − 1)(1 − sin²θ) = 1/k, find the value of k.
Prove that the points A(1,5), B(2,4) and C(9,−3) are collinear using distance formula.
Prove that √3 is an irrational number.
questions.answer: Assume √3=p/q where p,q are coprime integers. Then 3q²=p², so 3|p². By Euclid's lemma, 3|p. Let p=3m. Then q²=3m², so 3|q. Both p and q are divisible by 3, contradicting that they are coprime. Hence √3 is irrational.

questions.question: If α and β are zeroes of polynomial p(x) = x² + 3x + 1, find the value of (i) (α/β) + (β/α) and (ii) α² + β².
Prove that: (sinA − cosA + 1)/(sinA + cosA − 1) = 1/(secA − tanA)
questions.answer: RHS = 1/(secA−tanA). Multiply numerator and denominator by (secA+tanA): (secA+tanA)/(sec²A−tan²A)=(secA+tanA)/1=secA+tanA. LHS: divide by cosA: (tanA−1+secA)/(tanA+1−secA). = (tanA+secA−1)/(tanA−secA+1). Multiply N and D by (tanA+secA+1): ... after manipulation = secA+tanA = RHS. Proved.

questions.question: Arcs are drawn in square ABCD with A, B, C, D as centres of circles of radius 3.5 cm. If the area of square ABCD is 144 cm², find the perimeter of the shape PQRSTUVWP formed by arcs within the square.
questions.answer: Side of square=12 cm. Each arc is a quarter circle of radius 3.5 cm. The shape has 8 arcs — 4 from corners (quarter circle each) and 4 from midpoints. Total perimeter = 4 quarter-circles of r=3.5 + 4 quarter-circles of r=3.5 = 8 × (90/360) × 2π × 3.5 = 8 × (1/4) × 7π = 14π = 44 cm.

questions.question: A box contains 20 cards numbered from 1 to 20. Find the probability of getting: (i) a perfect square (ii) divisible by both 2 and 3 (iii) a prime number.
Which of the following is an irrational number?
The LCM of 2 and 3 is
State the Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic.
questions.answer: Every composite number can be expressed as a product of primes, and this factorization is unique, apart from the order in which the prime factors occur.

questions.question: If one zero of the polynomial p(x) = 5x² + 13x + k is the reciprocal of the other, then the value of k is
The pair of equations x = a and y = b graphically represents lines that are
The nature of roots of x² + 2x + 3 = 0 is
The nth term of an AP 5, 8, 11, 14, ... is
If △ABC ~ △PQR and AB/PQ = 3/4, then the ratio of their areas is
How many tangents can be drawn to a circle from a point inside the circle?
questions.answer: No tangent (zero tangents) can be drawn to a circle from a point inside the circle.

questions.question: Write the formula for the distance between two points (x₁,y₁) and (x₂,y₂).
If cos θ = 8/17, find the value of sin θ.
Write the formula for the volume of a cone of base radius r and height h.
A card is drawn at random from a well-shuffled pack of 52 cards. The probability that the card is a red queen is
The class mark of class interval 25-35 is
Write the formula for the area of a sector of a circle with radius r and angle θ (in degrees).
Find the LCM and HCF of 6, 72 and 120 by prime factorisation method.
If the sum of zeroes of the polynomial ax² + bx + c is equal to the product of zeroes, then find the relationship between a, b and c.
questions.answer: Sum of zeroes = -b/a. Product of zeroes = c/a. Given: -b/a = c/a. Therefore -b = c, i.e., b + c = 0.

questions.question: For what value of k will the pair of equations 3x + 4y = 12 and kx - 4y = 16 have a unique solution?
questions.answer: For unique solution: a₁/a₂ ≠ b₁/b₂. 3/k ≠ 4/(-4). 3/k ≠ -1. k ≠ -3. Any value of k except -3.

questions.question: Find the roots of the quadratic equation 3x² - 5x - 2 = 0 by factorisation.
In an AP, the first term is -5 and the last term is 45. If the sum of all terms is 120, find the number of terms.
In △ABC, DE ∥ BC. If AD = 4 cm, DB = 6 cm and AE = 2 cm, find EC.
questions.answer: By BPT: AD/DB = AE/EC. 4/6 = 2/EC. EC = 12/4 = 3 cm.

questions.question: Prove that tangents drawn at the ends of a diameter of a circle are parallel to each other.
questions.answer: Let AB be a diameter. Tangents at A and B are perpendicular to radius OA and OB respectively (tangent⊥radius). OA and OB are along the same line (diameter). So both tangents make 90° with the same line. Hence the two tangents are parallel to each other.

questions.question: Find the coordinates of the midpoint of the line segment joining points (3, 4) and (−1, 2).
Evaluate: sin²60° + 2tan45° − cos²30°
A ladder 15 m long leans against a wall making an angle of 60° with the ground. Find the height of the wall where the ladder rests.
Find the total surface area of a cylinder whose base radius is 7 cm and height is 10 cm.
Find the area of a sector of a circle with radius 14 cm and angle 90°. [π = 22/7]
Find the mode of the following data: 3, 5, 7, 5, 3, 5, 8, 5, 3, 7, 5.
Two dice are thrown simultaneously. Find the probability that the sum of numbers on top is 8.
Prove that 3 + 2√3 is irrational.
questions.answer: Assume 3+2√3 = p/q (rational). Then 2√3 = p/q−3 = (p−3q)/q. √3 = (p−3q)/(2q) — rational. But √3 is irrational — contradiction. Hence 3+2√3 is irrational.

questions.question: Prove that √2 + √3 is irrational.
questions.answer: Assume √2+√3=r (rational). Then r−√3=√2. Squaring: r²−2r√3+3=2. √3=(r²+1)/(2r) — rational. But √3 is irrational — contradiction. Hence √2+√3 is irrational.

questions.question: Find a quadratic polynomial, the sum and product of whose zeroes are −3 and 2 respectively. Hence find the zeroes.
questions.answer: p(x) = x²−(sum)x+product = x²+3x+2 = (x+1)(x+2). Zeroes: x=−1 and x=−2.

questions.question: 5 pencils and 7 pens together cost Rs.50, and 7 pencils and 5 pens together cost Rs.46. Find the cost of one pencil and one pen.
questions.answer: 5x+7y=50 …(1). 7x+5y=46 …(2). Adding: 12x+12y=96→x+y=8. Subtracting: 2x−2y=−4→x−y=−2. x=3, y=5. Pencil=Rs.3, Pen=Rs.5.

questions.question: Two water taps together can fill a tank in 9 3/8 hours. The larger tap takes 10 hours less than the smaller. Find the time each tap takes separately.
If the sum of first n terms of an AP is 3n² + 5n, find the nth term and the first term and common difference.
In a right triangle, prove that the square of the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of squares of the other two sides (Pythagoras theorem).
questions.answer: Given: △ABC right-angled at B. Draw BD⊥AC. △ABD~△ABC: AB/AC=AD/AB → AB²=AC×AD …(1). △BDC~△ABC: BC/AC=DC/BC → BC²=AC×DC …(2). Adding: AB²+BC²=AC(AD+DC)=AC². Proved.

questions.question: If the angle between two tangents drawn from an external point P to a circle of radius 5 cm is 60°, find the distance from P to the centre.
Find the area of the triangle with vertices A(1,2), B(3,−4) and C(5,6).
Prove that: (sinθ + cosθ)(tanθ + cotθ) = secθ + cosecθ
The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from a point on the ground, which is 30 m away from the foot of the tower, is 30°. Find the height of the tower.
A solid is in the shape of a cone on a hemisphere both with radius 7 cm. Height of cone is 14 cm. Find the total surface area.
The minute hand of a clock is 14 cm long. Find the area swept by the minute hand in 10 minutes.
Find the mean using step-deviation method: Class 10-30 (f=5), 30-50 (f=8), 50-70 (f=12), 70-90 (f=7), 90-110 (f=8). A=60.
A bag contains 5 red, 8 white and 7 black balls. A ball is drawn at random. Find probability that it is: (i) red (ii) not black (iii) black.
Solve graphically: 2x − y = 4 and x + y = 5. Also find the vertices of the triangle formed by the two lines and the x-axis.
questions.answer: Intersection: solve 2x-y=4 and x+y=5. Adding: 3x=9, x=3, y=2. x-axis cuts 2x-y=4 at (2,0); x+y=5 at (5,0). Triangle vertices: (3,2),(2,0),(5,0).

questions.question: An AP consists of 50 terms. The 3rd term is 12 and the last term is 106. Find the 29th term.
The sum of the areas of two squares is 468 m². If the difference of their perimeters is 24 m, find the sides of the two squares.
In a right triangle, the altitude from the right angle to the hypotenuse divides it into two triangles, each similar to the original. Prove.
questions.answer: In △ABD: ∠ADB=90°,∠A=∠A. In △ABC: ∠B=90°,∠A=∠A. By AA: △ABD~△ABC. In △BDC: ∠BDC=90°,∠C=∠C. By AA: △BDC~△ABC. Both similar to original.

questions.question: From a point Q, the length of the tangent to a circle is 24 cm and the distance from Q to the centre is 25 cm. Find the radius. Also find the area of the quadrilateral formed by the two tangents and radii.
questions.answer: r²+24²=25². r²=49. r=7cm. Quadrilateral area=2×(1/2)×r×tangent=2×(1/2)×7×24=168 cm².

questions.question: If (1,2), (4,y), (x,6) and (3,5) are vertices of a parallelogram taken in order, find x and y.
Prove that: (sinθ + cosθ)(tanθ + cotθ) = secθ + cosecθ

Two poles of equal heights stand on either side of a 80 m wide road. From a point between them on the road, angles of elevation of tops are 60° and 30°. Find the heights of the poles and distances from the point to each pole.
A metallic sphere of radius 10.5 cm is melted and recast into small cones each of radius 3.5 cm and height 3 cm. Find the number of cones formed.
A well of diameter 3 m is dug 14 m deep. The earth taken out is spread to form an embankment 4 m wide. Find the height of the embankment.
Draw less than ogive for: Marks 0-10(f=5), 10-20(f=10), 20-30(f=15), 30-40(f=12), 40-50(f=8). Find median.
One card is drawn from a pack of 52. Find probability: (i) king (ii) red king (iii) face card (iv) neither ace nor king.
A train covered a certain distance at uniform speed. If 10 km/h faster, takes 2 hours less. If 10 km/h slower, takes 3 hours more. Find the distance.
The sum of first 7 terms of an AP is 63 and the sum of its next 7 terms is 161. Find the 28th term.
From the top of a 7 m high building, angle of elevation of top of a cable tower is 60° and angle of depression of its foot is 45°. Determine the height of the tower.
A straight highway leads to the foot of a tower. A man on top observes a car at angle of depression 60°. After 6 sec car is observed at 30°. If tower height is 60 m, find speed of car.
